steps.title: Read the graph at the requested year
steps.content: The input $x$ is years after the 2000 census, so $2$ years after the census means $x=2$. The output $y$ is measured in thousands of people, so the graph value must be converted to people at the end.

steps.title: Estimate the y-value
steps.content: Trace straight up from $x=2$ until you hit the curve. The curve is a little above $4$ and clearly below $5$, about $4.3$ on the $y$-axis. Since the axis is in thousands, this means about $4.3$ thousand people.

steps.title: Convert and match the choice
steps.content: $4.3$ thousand people is $$4.3\times 1000=4300$$ people. Choice C, $4,300$, is the closest estimate.

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — '7,400': This would be y ≈ 7.4 on the graph, which is above the curve at x = 2. ✗
  • Choice B — '6,200': This would be y ≈ 6.2 on the graph, still above the curve at x = 2. ✗
  • Choice C — '4,300': The curve at x = 2 reads y ≈ 4.3, giving 4,300 people. ✓
  • Choice D — '3,000': This would be y = 3.0, which is below the curve at x = 2. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
